name: Rothschild & Ausbrooks, PLLC
description_short: Board-certified bankruptcy law firm serving Middle Tennessee for 30+ years, specializing in Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 relief for individuals facing debt, foreclosure, and wage garnishment.
description_long:
Rothschild & Ausbrooks, PLLC is an established bankruptcy law firm based in Goodlettsville, Tennessee, serving the Nashville and Middle Tennessee region. The firm has provided legal guidance and representation in consumer bankruptcy matters for more than three decades, building a reputation for experienced counsel in debt relief. The firm emphasizes having assisted thousands of individuals obtain relief from overwhelming debt through structured bankruptcy protection.

The firm offers legal representation for both Chapter 7 bankruptcy (fresh start liquidation) and Chapter 13 bankruptcy (reorganization with repayment plans). They specifically highlight Chapter 13's ability to halt collection activities, foreclosures, garnishments, repossessions, and levies while putting clients back on a course toward financial stability. The firm provides free initial consultations to evaluate clients' financial situations and develop appropriate debt relief strategies. Their services are designed for individuals struggling with hospital bills, credit card debt, and mortgage or auto payments they cannot sustain.

Rothschild & Ausbrooks distinguishes itself through board-certified attorney representation and emphasis on personalized service. The firm characterizes itself as dedicated, focused, genuine, communicative, knowledgeable, and experienced. They prioritize making the bankruptcy process less daunting by offering guidance and alleviating client stress throughout the legal proceedings. The firm positions debt relief as their stated top priority and claims to earn respect through offering high-quality bankruptcy services.

The primary limitation is that this is a specialized bankruptcy law firm with geographic focus on Middle Tennessee/Nashville area, making it inaccessible for consumers outside that region. Website content does not provide specific attorney credentials, fee structures, case outcomes, client testimonials, or detailed information about their board certification. The firm's website uses repetitive content blocks and lacks granular detail about Chapter 7 versus Chapter 13 strategy, timeline expectations, or specific success metrics that would inform consumer decision-making.

Consumers considering bankruptcy should also explore alternatives. Debt relief programs may negotiate settlements for less than owed, while debt consolidation loans can simplify payments. Credit counseling agencies offer free financial assessments. After bankruptcy, rebuilding credit through secured credit cards and credit builder loans provides a structured path back. Credit repair services can help ensure accurate reporting. After discharge, qualifying for an installment loan can begin rebuilding payment history on your credit report.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Board-certified attorney representation for bankruptcy matters
30+ years of experience counseling Middle Tennessee consumers
Free initial consultation to review financial situations
Specializes in both Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 bankruptcy options
Focuses specifically on stopping foreclosures, garnishments, repossessions, and collection activities
Claims to have assisted thousands of individuals achieve debt relief
Emphasizes personalized guidance and stress reduction throughout bankruptcy process
cons: Geographic limitation: serves only Nashville/Middle Tennessee area, not suitable for consumers outside the region
Website lacks specific attorney credentials, bar membership numbers, or board certification details to verify claims
No fee structure, pricing, or cost information disclosed on website
No client testimonials, case results, or success metrics provided to evaluate outcomes
Limited detail on Chapter 7 vs. Chapter 13 strategy, timelines, or what clients should realistically expect
best_for: Middle Tennessee residents facing foreclosure, wage garnishment, or repossession seeking Chapter 13 protection
Individuals with overwhelming credit card and medical debt seeking Chapter 7 fresh start relief
Consumers unable to meet living expenses who need legal guidance navigating bankruptcy options
diagnosis: Best for Middle Tennessee residents facing imminent foreclosure, wage garnishment, or vehicle repossession who need immediate legal protection through Chapter 13 bankruptcy, or those seeking fresh-start Chapter 7 relief. Primary caveat: geographic limitation to Nashville/Middle Tennessee area and lack of transparent fee information and specific attorney credentials make it necessary to contact directly for eligibility and cost confirmation.
services: Chapter 7 bankruptcy legal representation and filing
Chapter 13 bankruptcy legal representation and filing
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
